channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Warning signs your mail has been fraudulently redirected
item.description: Beware of change-of-address scams. Bad actors are impersonating others to request a change of address with the postal service to steal information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Generative AI has a long way to go as siloed data and abuse of its capacity remain a downside – but it does change the game for security teams
item.description: Generative AI enhances cybersecurity but faces challenges like siloed data, phishing threats, and a talent gap.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Kashmir's Sufi music lovers are sticking with the audio cassette
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — Farooq Ahmad Shaksaaz presses a button on his 1970 Sharp cassette player, and with a hefty clack the machine whirrs to life.
item.dc:creator: DAR YASIN and AIJAZ HUSSAIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cybersecurity officials warn against potentially costly Medusa ransomware attacks
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The FBI and the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency are warning against a dangerous ransomware scheme.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: This free web-based image editor gives Photoshop a run for its money
item.description: AI image editing may be all the rage, but good old-fashioned image editors are still essential. There’s a problem, though: Windows PCs, Chromebooks, and Macs don’t include exceptional image editors that go beyond the most basic editing needs.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: A massive SMS toll fee scam is sweeping the US – here’s how to stay safe, according to the FBI
item.description: A widespread SMS scam is targeting thousands of smartphone users in the US – here's how to stay safe.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Critics warn staff cuts at federal agencies overseeing US dams could put public safety at risk
item.description: Experts are warning that Trump administration workforce cuts at federal agencies overseeing U.S. dams are threatening their ability to provide reliable electricity, supply water to farmers and protect communities from floods.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bellisle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: JDK 25: The new features in Java 25
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 25, a planned long-term support release of standard Java due in September, now has two features officially proposed for it. The first is a preview of an API for stable values, a feature that promises to speed up the startup of Java applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Vance hopeful 'high-level' TikTok deal will be completed by early April
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Friday that he was hopeful a deal to keep TikTok operating in the U.S. will be wrapped up by the early April deadline.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Details of Nvidia's fastest video card ever leak; RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell GPU will have 96GB GDDR7 ECC memory
item.description: Details of Nvidia's fastest video card ever, the RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell GPU, have been spotted online.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Tesla says it is 'exposed' to retaliatory tariffs
item.description: Tesla warned U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer that the electric vehicle (EV) company and other American exporters are “inherently exposed” to retaliatory tariffs levied in response to ones from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: CAIOs are stepping out from the CIO’s shadow
item.description: In 2016, Andrew Ng, one of the best-known researchers in the field of AI, wrote about the benefits of establishing a chief AI officer role in companies, as well as the characteristics and responsibilities such a role should have.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Social media addicts: Here are tips to stop wasting time on apps
item.description: If you’ve got a smartphone, you probably spend too much time on it — checking Instagram, watching silly TikTok videos, messaging on WhatsApp or doomscrolling on X. It can be hard to curb excessive use of smartphones and social media, which are addictive by design.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: The future of AI isn’t the model—it’s the system
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: DOGE may be using an algorithm to fire federal workers
item.description: In the past month and a half, the Trump Administration has drastically reduced the federal government workforce. The cuts alone have generated concern and anger among workers and those who rely on US government services.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How AI-enabled ‘bossware’ is being used to track and evaluate your work
item.description: Employee monitoring software, also called “bossware” and “tattleware,” is increasingly being used to track and manage employees remotely via a business network or by using desktop software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google reportedly looking at smart glasses
item.description: After abandoning its Google Glass smart glasses in 2023, Google seems ready to take another kick at the can with what will reportedly be a $115 million acquisition of Canadian startup AdHawk Microsystems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Creating a cybersecurity standard of care: The future of software liability
item.description: This approach emphasizes the importance of integrating security measures into the software development lifecycle from the beginning. The post Creating a cybersecurity standard of care: The future of software liability first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Malware steals bank cards and passwords from millions of devices
item.description: One report claims at least 25 million people were targeted by infostealer malware between the beginning of 2023 and the end of 2024, capturing bank card details and passwords.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The Copyright Office takes on the sticky issue of artificial intelligence
item.description: The U.S. Copyright Office completed the second of two studies of AI and copyrights as it dealt with whether you can copyright outputs created using AI. The post The Copyright Office takes on the sticky issue of artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network
